name: Ascent to Lago de Engolasters — circular from Escaldes-Engordany
name_original: Subida al Lago de Engolasters — circular desde Escaldes-Engordany
region_intro: Dog friendly hiking trails in Vall Del Madriu-Perafita-Claror traverse a UNESCO World Heritage site characterized by its dramatic glacial landscapes. The region features soaring peaks, rugged mountains, and deep U-shaped valleys, including 26 glacial cirques. Numerous crystal-clear lakes and the Madriu River are prominent water features. The terrain varies from alpine meadows and highland forests to towering peaks and cascading waterfalls, offering a diverse environment for outdoor activities. Best dog friendly hiking trails in Vall Del Madriu-Perafita-Claror The most popular dog friendly hiking route is Andorra la Vella – Rec de l'Obac loop from Andorra la Vella, a 2.4 miles (3.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete. This easy route features minimal elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Anyós Riverside Picnic Area – Butterfly Tree loop from La Massana, a moderate 2.5 miles (4.0 km) path. This trail offers riverside scenery with a moderate ascent. Local hikers also love the Cap del Rec Refuge – Río de la Llosa loop from Refugi del Cap del Rec, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail leading through high mountain terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 58 minutes. Hiking in Vall Del Madriu-Perafita-Claror is defined by glacial formations, alpine meadows, and dense forests. The extensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Vall Del Madriu-Perafita-Claror are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1600 reviews. More than 14000 hikers have used komoot to explore Vall Del Madriu-Perafita-Claror's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the general rules for hiking with dogs in Vall del Madriu-Perafita-Claror?
A: Vall del Madriu-Perafita-Claror is a UNESCO World Heritage site, and while dogs are generally welcome, it's crucial to keep them under control, preferably on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Always clean up after your dog to preserve the pristine environment. Respect local regulations and signage, particularly around protected areas or private land.

Q: How many dog-friendly hiking trails are available in Vall del Madriu-Perafita-Claror?
A: There are 10 dog-friendly hiking trails listed in this guide for Vall del Madriu-Perafita-Claror, offering a range of difficulties and lengths to explore with your canine companion.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike with my dog in Vall del Madriu-Perafita-Claror?
A: The best time for hiking with your dog in Vall del Madriu-Perafita-Claror is generally spring or summer. During these seasons, wildflowers are in bloom, and the weather is ideal for outdoor activities. However, mountain weather can change quickly, so always be prepared with appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any easy dog-friendly hikes suitable for beginners or older dogs?
A: Yes, for an easy and pleasant walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40326015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andorra la Vella – Rec de l'Obac loop from Andorra la Vella&lt;/a&gt;. This route is just under 4 km with minimal elevation gain, making it perfect for a relaxed outing with your dog.

Q: Can I find any circular dog-friendly routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in Vall del Madriu-Perafita-Claror are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40326406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anyós Riverside Picnic Area – Butterfly Tree loop from La Massana&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 4 km loop.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on dog-friendly trails?
A: The valley boasts dramatic glacial landscapes, including soaring peaks, rugged mountains, and deep U-shaped valleys. You'll encounter numerous crystal-clear lakes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/952325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estany de la Nou&lt;/a&gt;, and the primary Madriu River. The diverse flora includes vast green pastures, wildflowers, and dense forests.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging dog-friendly hikes for experienced hikers?
A: For a more challenging adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10470526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap del Rec Refuge – Río de la Llosa loop from Refugi del Cap del Rec&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.7 km route with significant elevation changes, ideal for experienced hikers and their well-conditioned dogs.

Q: What historical or cultural points of interest can I discover with my dog?
A: The landscape holds traces of ancient shepherd paths, rustic stone huts (bordas), and terraced fields. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41554637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Route – Els Homes de Ferro sculptures loop from Ordino&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a glimpse into the region's mining history and traditional life.

Q: Are there any mountain huts or refuges along the dog-friendly trails?
A: Yes, mountain huts like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/952330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perafita Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3881947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontverd Refuge&lt;/a&gt; offer tranquil resting spots. While dogs might not be allowed inside all refuges, their surroundings provide excellent places for a break.

Q: What do other hikers say about dog-friendly trails in Vall del Madriu-Perafita-Claror?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Vall del Madriu-Perafita-Claror, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural beauty, the well-preserved environment, and the variety of trails suitable for different fitness levels, making it a rewarding experience for both hikers and their dogs.

Q: What should I pack for a dog-friendly hike in this region?
A: Besides your usual hiking gear, remember to bring essentials for your dog: a leash, water and a portable bowl, dog food/treats, waste bags, and possibly a dog first-aid kit. Sturdy boots and weather-appropriate clothing are recommended for yourself, as mountain conditions can change rapidly.
tips: At this point are the bordas —small stone shelters that housed farmers and ranchers— and the guardhouse of the Ràmio dam. This construction was carried out by the hydroelectric company FHASA, who promised to build roads in exchange for the exploitation of the waterfalls. This meant a great advance for the Andorrans, who in the middle of the 20th century had very poor living conditions.
Barbecue area in the forest. To recommend

Car park and hotel on one side (coming from Engolasters), and restaurant on the other side (coming from Encamp); in between several circular hiking trails on different levels; the path directly on the water is sometimes flooded depending on the level of the lake.

waypoints.name: Font dels Corralets Picnic and Barbecue Area
